project-memory.USER_DIRECTIVES: - In analyze-mode, gather context in parallel before deep work, consult Oracle or Artistry for complex problems, and synthesize findings before proceeding. - Apply fixes incrementally: one fix at a time, build, deploy, restart OpenCode, test in fresh conversation, confirm working before applying next fix. - User chooses which fix to apply next from the presented list — do not apply all fixes at once. - In search-mode, maximize search effort with parallel explore and librarian agents plus direct grep, ripgrep, and ast-grep; never stop at the first result. - When dispatching delegated tasks, always include `load_skills` and `run_in_background`. - For this cache-bust issue, test plugin-backed Claude models specifically because the user observes the problem with `opencode-antigravity-auth` models and not other providers. - Treat Antigravity Claude cache instability primarily as an `opencode-antigravity-auth` plugin issue; use Magic Context changes only for false-BUST reporting cleanup.
project-memory.CONFIG_DEFAULTS: - `quota_style_fallback` defaults to `false` in both the Zod schema (`src/plugin/config/schema.ts`) and `DEFAULT_CONFIG`; env override `OPENCODE_ANTIGRAVITY_QUOTA_STYLE_FALLBACK=1` - `quota_refresh_interval_minutes` defaults to 30 (increased from 15) - `max_account_switches` defaults to 2 (new config) - `empty_response_max_attempts` defaults to 2 (reduced from 4) - `antigravity-claude-sonnet-4-6-thinking` resolves to `claude-sonnet-4-6-thinking` and supports `low` (8192) and `max` (32768) reasoning variants. - `antigravity-gpt-oss-120b` resolves to `gpt-oss-120b` and supports the `medium` variant. - Antigravity Gemini 3 catalog uses `antigravity-gemini-3.5-flash` and `antigravity-gemini-3.1-pro`; `antigravity-gemini-3-flash` and `antigravity-gemini-3-pro` are removed. - `gemini-3-pro-preview` is removed from the plugin and opencode.json — Gemini 3 Pro is fully deprecated and no longer available on Antigravity or Gemini CLI. - `gemini-3.1-pro-high` is deprecated in the Antigravity API; `deprecatedModelIds` maps it to `gemini-pro-agent`. - The old `gemini-3-flash` ("Gemini 3 Flash") still exists as a separate model in the Antigravity API, distinct from Gemini 3.5 Flash. - Antigravity resolver maps `antigravity-gemini-3.5-flash` high/default tier to internal `gemini-3-flash-agent` and medium/low tiers to `gemini-3.5-flash-low`. The `gemini-cli` path fallback model is `gemini-3-flash-preview` since `gemini-3.5-flash` is missing from Gemini CLI quota buckets. The resolver recomputes `effectiveModel` when `thinkingLevel` changes. - `auto_resume` defaults to `true` in both the Zod schema (`src/plugin/config/schema.ts`) and `DEFAULT_CONFIG`.
project-memory.CONSTRAINTS: - `isValidRequestPart()` in `src/plugin/request.ts` allows parts with these keys only: `text`, `functionCall`, `functionResponse`, `inlineData`, `fileData`, `executableCode`, `codeExecutionResult`, `thought`. Parts lacking any of these keys are replaced with `{ text: "." }` sentinels — adding a new valid part type requires updating this allowlist. - When extending `systemInstruction` in `src/plugin/request.ts`, append new `{ text }` parts to the parts array rather than mutating the existing text string, to avoid corrupting cached prefix text. - Anthropic `thinking` field in message content blocks can be a nested object (from round-tripped conversation history), not just a string. `getThinkingPartText()` in `src/plugin/request.ts` handles both `part.text` (Gemini-style thought) and `part.thinking` (Anthropic-style) for safe string extraction; do not use raw `||` property chains. - `JSON.stringify` silently drops keys with `undefined` values. Never assign `thinking: undefined` — always use `thinking: ""` or omit the field entirely. - The Pydantic error `messages.N.content.M.thinking.thinking: Field required` means the `thinking` TEXT FIELD is MISSING ENTIRELY from a block that matched the "thinking" union variant — NOT that there is a nested `thinking: { thinking: ... }` object. - Sentinel blocks must NOT use thinking/thought format (`{ thought: true }` or `{ type: "thinking" }`) — the Antigravity proxy converts these to Claude thinking blocks and may drop required fields when text is empty. - Sentinel text blocks must contain non-whitespace text; Claude rejects whitespace-only text blocks and the proxy drops empty strings. - Antigravity Claude requests must not include `cache_control` at top-level or part-level because the Google sandbox proxy rejects both as unknown fields. - Never use `.filter()` on `contents` or `parts` arrays in the prompt-cached prefix or system-instruction parts. Use `.map()` with `{ text: "." }` sentinels to preserve array indices and prevent cache hash mismatches. Implemented in `sanitizeRequestPayloadForAntigravity()` in `src/plugin/request.ts` and `stripAllThinkingBlocks()` in `src/plugin/request-helpers.ts`. - `gemini-2.5-flash-image` and `gemini-2.5-flash-image-preview` require the Google AI API path, not Antigravity OAuth.
project-memory.ARCHITECTURE_DECISIONS: - Quota style fallback (switching from antigravity to gemini-cli on exhaustion) retries the full request on a different quota pool, causing double-spending; disabling it saves quota at the cost of fewer retry paths - Endpoint fallback chain uses 2 endpoints (skips autopush), down from 3 - `ensureClaudeMaxOutputTokens` in `src/plugin/transform/claude.ts` uses `computeClaudeMaxOutputTokens(budget)` instead of hardcoded 64K constant - `apiRequestCount` in `src/plugin.ts` tracks API calls per user message and is logged via `pushDebug` when retry loops occur. - `safeStringify()` in `request.ts` wraps `JSON.stringify` with a replacer function that flattens any non-string `thinking` field to `""` at serialization time, while preserving legitimate Schema objects in `tools`/`function_declarations` (detected by `JSON_SCHEMA_TYPES` set containing standard JSON Schema types). - Claude request sanitization in `src/plugin/request.ts` rewrites empty or whitespace-only Anthropic `messages[].content[].text` blocks to `"."` before serialization so Claude accepts the request. - OpenCode activates the Antigravity Google auth/fetch path only when `auth.json` contains a stored `google` OAuth entry; without it, native `@ai-sdk/google` initializes and requires an API key. - Thinking-replacement sentinels use `{ text: "." }` with inherited `cache_control` for two reasons: (1) empty text is dropped by the Antigravity proxy and whitespace-only text is rejected by Claude; (2) OpenCode persists transformed responses and replays them into later request history, so sentinels must survive the round-trip as valid content. - The Antigravity plugin activates its fetch interceptor path only when OpenCode's `getAuth()` returns an OAuth credential (`isOAuthAuth(auth)` — checks `type === "oauth"`). If no valid OAuth auth exists, the loader clears stale account storage and returns `{}`, bypassing the intercept path entirely. - Thinking warmup is opt-in (config `thinking_warmup: false` default in `src/plugin/config/schema.ts` and `DEFAULT_CONFIG`); the warmup function only runs when explicitly enabled, saving one full API call per session. - Capacity retries are capped at `MAX_CAPACITY_RETRIES = 1` in `src/plugin.ts` (previously 3), reducing redundant API calls on quota exhaustion. - Claude thinking models default to `.medium` tier (16,384 thinking tokens) in `src/plugin/transform/model-resolver.ts` when no variant suffix is specified; `.low` (8,192), `.medium` (16,384), and `.high` (32,768) variants remain available via explicit suffix. - `computeClaudeMaxOutputTokens(thinkingBudget)` in `src/plugin/transform/claude.ts` returns `thinkingBudget + 16384`, replacing the previous hardcoded `CLAUDE_THINKING_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS = 64000` for dynamic output token caps. - Claude models (`isClaudeModel()` in `src/plugin/request.ts`) reject assistant message prefill. When `contents` or `messages` ends on a model/assistant turn, the plugin appends `{ role: "user", parts: [{ text: "[Continue]" }] }` to both array paths. - `src/plugin/request.ts` defines `SENTINEL_SIGNATURE = "skip_thought_signature_validator"` (distinct from the imported `SKIP_THOUGHT_SIGNATURE` constant). On the thinking-signature restoration path, `ensureThoughtSignature()` injects `SENTINEL_SIGNATURE` into `thoughtSignature`/`signature` fields of thinking parts that lack a valid signature. - `sanitizeThinkingInMessages()` in `src/plugin/request.ts` wraps `deepSanitizeThinkingFields()` to scope recursion to `contents`, `messages`, `system`, and `systemInstruction` only — never `tools`/`function_declarations` — because tool schemas can legitimately contain `thinking` properties (e.g., `google_search`'s `thinking: {type: "boolean"}`). Both `JSON.stringify` call sites use the scoped wrapper. - `signatureCache` prunes expired session maps and caps the outer session map at 10 sessions to avoid one-entry-per-session RAM growth. - OpenCode v1.14.41 internal Codex OAuth plugin returns non-empty `OAUTH_DUMMY_KEY = "opencode-oauth-dummy-key"` from auth loader (`apiKey: OAUTH_DUMMY_KEY`) before replacing auth headers. opencode-antigravity-auth currently returns `apiKey: ""`, which can trip Desktop/@ai-sdk provider initialization before fetch interception. - `cache_control` markers exist at two levels in the transform pipeline: part-level (preserved through all transforms) and nested-inside-content (stripped when content is extracted to a string). Part-level markers are OpenCode's caching breakpoints and must survive the transform pipeline. - OpenCode's `applyCaching()` marks the first 2 system messages and the last 2 non-system messages with `cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" }`. The provider cache hash covers `[tools]+[system]+[messages up to each breakpoint]`. - Magic context's `tags` table in `context.db` uses a SQLite trigger to upsert duplicate `(session_id, tag_number)` inserts because the native addon issues plain inserts. - `account_selection_strategy` (config: `src/plugin/config/schema.ts`) defaults to `"hybrid"` (health score + token bucket + LRU freshness). Also accepts `"sticky"` (same account until rate-limited; preserves prompt cache) and `"round-robin"` (rotate every request). - `atomicWriteFile()` in `src/plugin/storage.ts` writes to a hex-suffixed `.tmp` file, retries `fs.rename()` up to 3 times with 50/100/200ms backoff on `EPERM`/`EACCES`, then falls back to `fs.copyFile()` + `fs.unlink()`. Both `saveAccounts()` and `saveAccountsReplace()` use this helper. - Response-path transformers `transformGeminiCandidate()` and `transformThinkingParts()` in `src/plugin/request-helpers.ts` and `deduplicateThinkingText()` in `src/plugin/core/streaming/transformer.ts` construct output objects with only explicit fields — never `{ ...spread }` — to prevent the original `thinking: <object>` from leaking into output. - `src/plugin/cache/signature-cache.ts` uses sync temp-file + rename pattern (`writeFileSync` → `.tmp`, `renameSync` to target, fallback `writeFileSync` from `readFileSync(tmpPath)`) for atomic cache persistence. - `stripAllThinkingBlocks()` and `removeTrailingThinkingBlocks()` in `src/plugin/request-helpers.ts` use `.map()` (not `.filter()`) to replace thinking parts with `{ text: ".", cache_control? }` sentinels, preserving array indices for prompt-cache stability. - Plugin intercepts `fetch()` at Phase 3, after oh-my-openagent hooks (Phase 1), magic-context injection (Phase 1), and OpenCode's `applyCaching()` (Phase 2). Any message mutation at Phase 3 risks busting the provider prompt cache. - `claude_tool_hardening` (config: `src/plugin/config/schema.ts`, default: `true`) injects parameter signatures into tool descriptions and adds a system instruction for tool call reliability. Disable via `claude_tool_hardening: false` to reduce context size. - All model definitions, resolver mappings, and quota classifications are defined in `src/plugin/model-registry.ts`, with `src/plugin/config/models.ts` acting as a re-export surface. - At startup, `src/plugin.ts` calls `initAntigravityVersion` from `src/plugin/version.ts` to dynamically resolve the latest Antigravity version from the auto-updater API, changelog page, or a fallback constant. - Authentication setup in `src/plugin.ts` uses `detectAuthStorageDrift` from `src/plugin/auth-drift.ts` to restore missing or invalid OpenCode OAuth configuration from the local account storage. - The build script compiles with `tsc` and bundles `index.ts` to `dist/index.js` via esbuild as an ESM Node bundle, externalizing `@openauthjs/*`, `proper-lockfile`, and `zod` while bundling internal source and `@opencode-ai/plugin`. - By default, all plugin models route requests through the Antigravity proxy (`headerStyle: "antigravity"`) unless `cli_first` is set to `true` in the configuration. - `deduplicateThinkingText` in src/plugin/core/streaming/transformer.ts filters out duplicate thinking chunks by returning `[]` inside `flatMap`, preventing the emission of blank text blocks to OpenCode. - In src/plugin/storage.ts, `proper-lockfile` is dynamically imported and cached in `_cachedLock` inside `getLockFunction()` to avoid CJS/ESM default-export interop issues. - `ensureThinkingFields` in src/plugin/request.ts recursively traverses payloads before serialization, adding `thinking: ""` to `type: "thinking"` blocks and `text: ""` to `thought: true` blocks if missing or non-string, preventing `JSON.stringify` from omitting them. - ensureThinkingFields in src/plugin/request.ts recursively traverses payloads before serialization, adding thinking: "" to type: "thinking" blocks and text: "" to thought: true blocks if missing or non-string, preventing JSON.stringify from omitting them. - deduplicateThinkingText in src/plugin/core/streaming/transformer.ts filters out duplicate thinking chunks by returning [] inside flatMap, preventing the emission of blank text blocks to OpenCode. - In src/plugin/storage.ts, proper-lockfile is dynamically imported and cached in _cachedLock inside getLockFunction() to avoid CJS/ESM default-export interop issues. - The build script compiles with tsc and bundles index.ts to dist/index.js via esbuild as an ESM Node bundle, externalizing @openauthjs/*, proper-lockfile, and zod while bundling internal source and @opencode-ai/plugin. - By default, all plugin models route requests through the Antigravity proxy (headerStyle: "antigravity") unless cli_first is set to true in the configuration. - The gemini-2.5-flash model is functional on the Antigravity daily sandbox endpoint (ANTIGRAVITY_ENDPOINT_DAILY) despite being excluded from fetchAvailableModels responses. - gemini-3-pro-image-preview is omitted from the plugin's model registry because it is unavailable on the Antigravity proxy endpoints. - All model definitions, resolver mappings, and quota classifications are defined in src/plugin/model-registry.ts, with src/plugin/config/models.ts acting as a re-export surface. - At startup, src/plugin.ts calls initAntigravityVersion from src/plugin/version.ts to dynamically resolve the latest Antigravity version from the auto-updater API, changelog page, or a fallback constant. - Authentication setup in src/plugin.ts uses detectAuthStorageDrift from src/plugin/auth-drift.ts to restore missing or invalid OpenCode OAuth configuration from the local account storage. - CLIProxyAPI v6.9.x removed server-side `ANTIGRAVITY_SYSTEM_INSTRUCTION` injection. The plugin does not inject it either.
project-memory.ENVIRONMENT: - `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth` v1.6.22 is published to npm and pushed/tagged on GitHub. It includes auth menu UX polish: EXHAUSTED quota shows 'resets in ?h' fallback, EXHAUSTED skips redundant '0%' suffix, 100% READY groups hidden from quota summary, consistent EXHAUSTED display across badge and plain-text formatters. - Local workspace is at `D:\Projects\opencode-antigravity-auth` - `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth` v1.6.21 is published to npm and pushed/tagged on GitHub. It includes 12 quota optimization enhancements: thinking warmup opt-in (default OFF), capacity retries capped at 1, default thinking tier medium (16K), dynamic maxOutputTokens, API request counter, endpoint fallbacks reduced to 2, empty response retries reduced to 2, account switch cap (max_account_switches: 2), quota refresh interval 30 min, ensureClaudeMaxOutputTokens dynamic, and quota_style_fallback OFF by default (no double-pool spending). - Build output lands in `dist/src/` (mirrors `src/` structure). - GitHub repo `https://github.com/expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth` is the active origin fork containing the `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth` scoped-package changes and upstream NoeFabris merges. - Desktop/Electron strict Node ESM rejects extensionless relative imports (e.g., `./src/plugin`); TUI/Bun tolerates them. - Local installed Magic Context copies in the OpenCode package cache and config `node_modules` contain a manual `message.updated.no_usage` patch for usage-less zero-token `message.updated` events. - `C:\Users\Expiren\.config\opencode\antigravity.json` sets `claude_tool_hardening=false`. - `proper-lockfile` v4.1.2 is CJS; exports both `module.exports = lock` and `module.exports.lock = lock`. - Magic context log file: `C:\Users\Expiren\AppData\Local\Temp\magic-context.log`. - Magic context SQLite database: `C:\Users\Expiren\.local\share\opencode\storage\plugin\magic-context\context.db`. - Antigravity user config path: `C:\Users\Expiren\.config\opencode\antigravity.json`. - Magic Context user config path: `C:\Users\Expiren\.config\opencode\magic-context.jsonc`. - OpenCode config loads `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@latest`. - npm is logged in as `expiren`; publishing requires an npm automation or granular access token with bypass-2FA enabled. - Local workspace is at `D:\Projects\opencode-antigravity-auth`. - `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth` v1.6.18 is published to npm and pushed/tagged on GitHub. It includes the team-mode batch: explicit Repair auth menu action, auth doctor/current diagnostics expansion, central model registry, quota-aware auth menu hints/status module, runtime Antigravity version diagnostics, GeminiCLI UA header identity matching, and fingerprint restore/history menu UX. - `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth` v1.6.19 is published to npm and pushed/tagged on GitHub. It includes: auth switch with restart suggestion, auth current standalone command, per-model quota status labels ([READY]/[WAIT]/[COOLDOWN]), and consolidated duplicate appendClaudeThinkingHint call (removed from request.ts, kept in claude.ts only). - @expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth v1.6.19 is published to npm and pushed/tagged on GitHub. It includes: auth switch with restart suggestion, auth current standalone command, per-model quota status labels ([READY]/[WAIT]/[COOLDOWN]), and consolidated duplicate appendClaudeThinkingHint call (removed from request.ts, kept in claude.ts only). - @expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth v1.6.18 is published to npm and pushed/tagged on GitHub. It includes the team-mode batch: explicit Repair auth menu action, auth doctor/current diagnostics expansion, central model registry, quota-aware auth menu hints/status module, runtime Antigravity version diagnostics, GeminiCLI UA header identity matching, and fingerprint restore/history menu UX.
project-memory.WORKFLOW_RULES: - When debugging persistent issues across many fix iterations, reset to a known-working baseline and apply fixes one at a time with user testing between each. - For Claude cache-stability experiments, run controlled A/B tests: baseline current behavior, then `claude_tool_hardening=false`, then sticky/cache-first account settings. - Deploy: `npm run build` then `npm publish --access public` publishes `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth` to npm. OpenCode loads it from `C:\Users\Expiren\.cache\opencode\packages\@expiren\` via `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@latest`. - When using background bash tasks (`bash({ background: true })`) in TUI mode, run OpenCode as `opencode --port 0` to receive completion reminders; background bash notifications are unavailable without `--port 0` due to anomalyco/opencode#28202. OpenCode Desktop does not need special treatment. Prefer foreground bash or ensure TUI uses `--port 0` when relying on background bash completion reminders. - OpenCode bug anomalyco/opencode#28202: background bash notifications require running TUI as `opencode --port 0`; OpenCode Desktop does not need special treatment. Prefer foreground bash or ensure TUI uses `--port 0` when relying on background bash completion reminders.
project-memory.KNOWN_ISSUES: - OpenCode `providerOptions`/`cache_control` markers may be consumed before the fetch body, so the Antigravity proxy can receive no explicit cache-control markers from OpenCode. - Latest OpenCode versions may cause `/provider` and `/config/providers` to return 400 when the Antigravity plugin is loaded (GitHub issues #26546, #26547). Adding provider-model metadata fields (`id`, `release_date`, `attachment`, `reasoning`, `temperature`, `tool_call`) is required — the plugin is currently in active config with these fields populated in all model definitions. - Claude thinking-hint injection has two call sites, `src/plugin/transform/claude.ts` and `src/plugin/request.ts`; verify whether both fire on the same Claude thinking request before consolidating to one implementation. - `gemini-3.5-flash-preview` is missing from `OPENCODE_MODEL_DEFINITIONS`, so Gemini 3.5 Flash lacks a defined gemini-cli preview path even though the resolver can generate that fallback id. - `gemini-3.1-flash-image` and `gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview` are available on Gemini API, Vertex AI, and Antigravity with image output, but the plugin lacks explicit `OPENCODE_MODEL_DEFINITIONS` entries for them. - TypeScript LSP diagnostics can fail with server exit `-4058`; use `npm run typecheck` as the reliable TypeScript validation path. - Magic context Phase 1 stripping, placeholder replay, and compartment-agent activity can cause alternating cache misses even when the plugin request pipeline is stable. - If magic context recreates `context.db`, the duplicate-tag upsert trigger must be re-added. - Plugin-backed Claude models are more cache-fragile than other providers because Claude tool hardening, interleaved-thinking hints, and tool-description signature injection can change the prompt prefix. - Local `opencode run` may fail before model execution with a stale `Session not found` reference to `ses_2a1a871c1ffecZaQ5XpN53G1a1`; use a fresh OpenCode TUI session for live Claude validation if that occurs. - Upstream npm package `opencode-antigravity-auth` is archived with unfixed bugs; OpenCode Desktop resolves and caches it separately from local builds. - Desktop cannot use the local plugin fork — the plugin fetch interceptor does not activate for Desktop's request path regardless of dummy-key or local-wrapper approaches, causing "API key not valid" errors. - Conversations with persisted `thinking: <object>` history keep failing with `messages.*.content.*.thinking.thinking: Field required`; test fixes in a fresh conversation after restarting OpenCode. - The Antigravity account manager lacks a "Use this account" restore action, so existing stored accounts cannot recreate missing OpenCode `google` OAuth auth without re-running OAuth. - Antigravity Claude prompt-cache markers are unsupported on the current proxy path; enabling Claude auto-caching causes invalid JSON payload 400s. - Magic Context guidance injection (`hephaestus`, `sisyphus`, `sisyphus-junior`, or generic guidance) can change the system prompt hash and cause cache busts even after the v0.14.1 sentinel fix. - Account rotation can split provider cache buckets by changing auth identity, managed project, fingerprint, or headers; sticky account selection improves prompt-cache stability when quota allows. - OpenCode Desktop may not expose plugin OAuth methods for Google and can fall back to the built-in Google API-key connect screen even when TUI shows the Antigravity OAuth method. - Antigravity API rejects explicit Claude `cache_control` markers, so normal Anthropic cache breakpoints are unavailable. - `appendClaudeThinkingHint()` in `src/plugin/transform/claude.ts` mutates the last system text part, destabilizing Claude prefix cache — should append a separate `{ text: hint }` part instead. - Magic Context plugin counts `message.updated` events with `hasUsageTokens=false` as cache BUSTs, inflating BUST diagnostics for Antigravity Claude models — fix needed in Magic Context to ignore zero-usage events. - The plugin's gemini-cli path routes to the Antigravity proxy (`cloudcode-pa.googleapis.com`) with nodejs-client UA headers, NOT to the public Google API — gemini-cli models may only work if the proxy recognizes their model IDs. Manual `/v1internal` API testing returns 401 even for known-working models, so live OpenCode testing is the only reliable verification method. - OpenCode Desktop v1.14.41 can fall back to native `@ai-sdk/google` even when `auth.json` contains `google` OAuth and TUI works, causing `Google Generative AI API key is missing`. This likely happens because Desktop's utility-process server bypasses the plugin's auth-loader `fetch` override.

compartment.title: Investigated quota exhaustion causes after Antigravity 2.0 update

compartment: Investigated why Antigravity Claude/Gemini quota exhausts fast after the Antigravity 2.0 update. Conducted exhaustive parallel code analysis (retry patterns, token bloat, warmup, account rotation, thinking budgets) and external search. Found both plugin-side issues (thinking warmup consuming an extra full API call per session, retry loops multiplying a single user message into up to 9+ API calls, ~700–3000 extra tokens per request from tool hardening and forced 64K maxOutputTokens) and Antigravity-side factors (possible tighter quota allocations). No code changes were made — the user requested analysis only. Assistant provided config-level mitigations (disable tool hardening — already set, sticky account selection, reduce empty response retries, lower max rate limit wait) and proposed code enhancements (opt-in warmup, cap capacity retries, lower default thinking tier).

compartment.title: Agreed to proceed with quota-saving enhancements

compartment: Recapped the quota-exhaustion analysis findings after a stale background agent timeout. User greenlit implementation of the proposed enhancements. Assistant asked which of the 5 ranked options to tackle first. No code changes have been made yet — awaiting user's prioritization. U: start dowin the inhancement User committed to implementing the proposed quota-saving enhancements. The assistant requested a priority selection from the 5 options (opt-in thinking warmup, capacity retries cap, lower default thinking tier, reduced maxOutputTokens, request counter). Implementation is deferred pending user choice.

After the user greenlit all 5 proposed enhancements simultaneously, the assistant implemented: thinking warmup opt-in (default false), capacity retries cap reduced to 1, default thinking tier lowered to medium (16,384), dynamic maxOutputTokens computed as `thinkingBudget + 16384`, and per-message API request counter. Typecheck is clean and all 1,124 tests pass.

compartment.title: Rechecked remaining quota waste and clarified thinking budget behavior

compartment: Answered the thinking budget question: default is 16384 (medium), but `.high` suffix or `variant.thinkingBudget` config override yields 32768; `.low` yields 8192. Conducted a thorough recheck of all remaining quota-waste patterns across the codebase. Identified 6 patterns: endpoint fallback loop in `plugin.ts` (up to 3x per request when daily→autopush→prod all fail), empty response retries at default 4 attempts, account rotation re-sends (1 per switch), Google Search tool calls (separate API call per invocation), quota check calls (`checkAccountsQuota()`), and dead code path `ensureClaudeMaxOutputTokens` in `claude.ts` (uses hardcoded constant but `applyClaudeTransforms` is never called from `request.ts` — dynamic `computeClaudeMaxOutputTokens` is the active path). Recommended reducing endpoint fallbacks from 3 to 2 (skip autopush) and `empty_response_max_attempts` from 4 to 2. The previous 5 quota-saving enhancements (thinking warmup opt-in, capacity retries cap at 1, default thinking tier medium, dynamic maxOutputTokens, request counter) are confirmed active.

compartment.title: Fixed all 6 remaining quota waste patterns

compartment: Implemented the 6 remaining fixable quota waste patterns: removed autopush from endpoint fallback chain (3→2 endpoints), reduced `empty_response_max_attempts` from 4 to 2, added `max_account_switches: 2` config with enforcement in the retry loop, increased `quota_refresh_interval_minutes` from 15 to 30, and replaced the hardcoded 64K in `ensureClaudeMaxOutputTokens` with dynamic `computeClaudeMaxOutputTokens()`. Updated 6 test expectations from 64K to the dynamic value (32K for low budget). All 1,124 tests pass across 42 test files. Worst-case API calls per user message dropped from ~12 to ~5. U: fix the "🟡 Remaining Waste (fixable but lower priority) " all of them User directed to fix all 6 remaining lower-priority quota waste patterns simultaneously. All implemented, tested, and passing.

compartment.title: Rechecked root causes of fast quota exhaustion

compartment: Conducted thorough re-trace of every fetch call and retry path after all 11 fixes. Confirmed no remaining loop-level waste — normal operation is 1-2 API calls per message. Identified the real root cause of fast exhaustion: token consumption per message (thinking tokens at 16K + output tokens at ~8K = ~24K per message, plus full conversation history context), not retry multipliers. Flagged quota-fallback double-spending (switching header styles retries the full large request on a different quota pool) as the largest remaining actionable concern. Recommended adding a config option to disable quota style fallback entirely.

compartment.title: Implemented quota_style_fallback config option (OFF by default)

compartment: Greenlit and implemented a `quota_style_fallback` config option (default: `false`) to eliminate double-pool quota spending when Gemini is rate-limited. Added the boolean to the Zod schema (`src/plugin/config/schema.ts`) with `@default false` and env override `OPENCODE_ANTIGRAVITY_QUOTA_STYLE_FALLBACK`, included in `DEFAULT_CONFIG`. Wired into `resolveHeaderRoutingDecision()` in `plugin.ts` so `allowQuotaFallback = family === "gemini" && config.quota_style_fallback`. Fixed 4 existing tests that passed partial configs without the field (added `?? false` fallback), and added 2 new tests (opt-in enabled path, Claude never fallback). All 1,126 tests pass. With this change, Gemini rate-limited requests only rotate accounts — never re-send across both antigravity and gemini-cli pools.
CONFIG_DEFAULTS: * `quota_style_fallback` defaults to `false` in both the Zod schema (`src/plugin/config/schema.ts`) and `DEFAULT_CONFIG`; env override `OPENCODE_ANTIGRAVITY_QUOTA_STYLE_FALLBACK=1`
